a: Have you ever been to California?
Have you ever run a marathon?

q: (speak / famous person?) Write a question using 'ever'.
a: Have you ever spoken to a famous person?

q: (most beautiful place / visit?) Write a question using 'ever'.
a: What is the most beautiful place you have ever visited?

q: Are you hungry? No, I ___ (not / eat) much today.
a: Are you hungry? No, I haven't eaten much today.

q: Can you play chess? Yes, but I ___ (not / play) it for ages.
a: Can you play chess? Yes, but I haven't played it for ages.

q: Are you enjoying your holiday? Yes, it is the best holiday I ___ (have) for a long time.
a: Are you enjoying your holiday? Yes, it is the best holiday I've had for a long time.

q: What is that book about? I don't know. I ___ (not / read) it.
a: What is that book about? I don't know. I haven't read it.

q: Is Brussels an interesting place? I have no idea. I ___ (never / be) there.
a: Is Brussels an interesting place? I have no idea. I've never been there.

q: Do you like caviar? I don't know. I ___ (never / try) it.
a: Do you like caviar? I don't know. I've never tried it.

q: Mike was late for work again today. Again? He ___ (be) late every day this week.
a: Mike was late for work again today. Again? He has been late every day this week.

q: Who is that woman by the door? I don't know. I ___ (never / see) her before.
a: Who is that woman by the door? I don't know. I've never seen her before.

q: Use 'I haven't' and 'travelled by bus' to write a sentence about yourself with 'recently'.
a: I haven't travelled by bus recently.

q: Some children at the zoo are looking at a giraffe. They have never seen one before. (It's the first time...)
a: It's the first time they have seen a giraffe.

q: Sue is riding a horse. She does not look confident and she has never ridden before. (It's the first time...)
a: It's the first time she has ridden a horse.

q: Joe and Lisa are in Japan. They were in Japan once before. (This is the second time...)
a: This is the second time they have been to Japan.

q: Emily is staying at the Prince Hotel. She stayed there a few years ago. (It's not the first time...)
a: It's not the first time she has stayed at this hotel.

q: Ben is playing tennis for the first time. He is a complete beginner. (It's the first time...)
a: It's the first time he has played tennis.
